chapterTitle: Chapter 6: Rendering Strategies & Data Fetching in Next.js

MCQs.question: What is the default rendering mode for components in Next.js App Router?
Which fetch option makes a request never cached (always fresh)?
What does this code do?

options.text: Caches data and revalidates after 60 seconds

MCQs.question: Which environment variable prefix makes it accessible in Client Components?
What function generates static paths at build time for dynamic routes?
How do you force a page to always render dynamically (SSR)?

options.text: Triggers on-demand revalidation of all fetches with that tag
Adds a tag to HTML elements

MCQs.question: When should you use SSG (Static Site Generation)?

options.text: For static content like about pages and documentation

ShortQuestions.question: Explain the difference between SSR, SSG, and ISR. When would you use each rendering strategy?
How do you fetch data in Server Components? Show examples of fetching with different caching strategies (default, no-cache, revalidate).
What is generateStaticParams? How do you use it to pre-render dynamic routes at build time?
Explain environment variables in Next.js. What's the difference between NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables and regular environment variables? Which are accessible where?
Create a blog page that: 1) Pre-renders all posts at build time, 2) Revalidates every 10 minutes, 3) Allows on-demand revalidation via API route.
